revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[User:Immanuelle/sandbox]] to [[User:Munakata Faith]]

[[WP:AES|←]]Removed redirect to [[Template:Munakata Faith]]
revision.text: # [[Asahikawa Shrine]] # [[Ebetsu shrine|Ebetsu Shrine]] # [[Hakodate Hachiman Shrine]] # [[Hokkaidō Gokoku Shrine]] # [[Hokkaidō Shrine|Hokkaidō Jingu]] # [[Hokkaidō Tōshō-gū]] # [[Hokumon Shrine]] # [[Itsukushima shrine (Kushiro)|Itsukushima Shrine]] # [[Iwamizawa Shrine]] # [[Kamikawa Shrine]] # [[Nishino Shrine]] # [[Obihiro Shrine]] # [[Ōta Jinja (Setana)]] # [[Sapporo Hachimangū]] # [[Shiraoi Hachiman Shrine]] # [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)|Sumiyoshi Shrine]] # [[Tarumaezan Shrine]]

.<ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj026.htm Section XIX.—The Palace of Suga.]</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-of-the-Great-Land.]</ref>
<ref>“‘My Own Inari’: Personalization of the Deity in Inari Worship.” ''Japanese Journal of Religious Studies'' 23, no. 1/2 (1996): 87-88</ref>
546<ref>Anston, p. 142 (Vol. 1)</ref>–510 BC<ref name=":93">Anston, p. 143 (Vol. 1)</ref>

<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |last=文也 |date=2019-05-26 |title=仲姫命とはどんな人？ |url=https://rekishi.sseikatsu.net/nakahime/ |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=歴史好きブログ |language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 |title=仲姫命(なかつひめのみこと)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%B2%E5%A7%AB%E5%91%BD-1096807 |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>

<!--citations from [[Nakatsuhime]]--><ref name="Nakatsuhime:0">{{Cite web |last=文也 |date=2019-05-26 |title=仲姫命とはどんな人？ |url=https://rekishi.sseikatsu.net/nakahime/ |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=歴史好きブログ |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="Nakatsuhime:1">{{Cite web |last=日本人名大辞典+Plus |first=朝日日本歴史人物事典,デジタル版 |title=仲姫命(なかつひめのみこと)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BB%B2%E5%A7%AB%E5%91%BD-1096807 |access-date=2023-01-19 |website=コトバンク |language=ja}}</ref>

According to the ''Kiki'', Empress Jingū gave birth to a baby boy whom she named ''Homutawake'' (aka [[Emperor Ōjin]]) following her return from Korean conquest. The legend alleges that her son was conceived but unborn when Emperor Chūai died. As three more years would pass before ''Homutawake'' was finally born, this claim appears to be mythical and symbolic rather than real. Scholar [[William George Aston]] has suggested that this claim was misinterpreted, and instead refers to a period of less than nine months containing three "years" (some seasons), e.g. three [[Harvest|harvests]].<ref>[[William George Aston|Aston, William]]. (1998). ''Nihongi'', Vol. 1, pp. 224–253.</ref> If Ōjin was an actual historical figure then historians have proposed that he ruled later than attested years of 270 to 310 AD.<ref name="Kofun">{{cite web |author=Kelly, Charles F. |title=Kofun Culture |url=http://www.t-net.ne.jp/~keally/kofun.html |access-date=November 4, 2019 |work=www.t-net.ne.jp}}</ref><ref>{{cite book |author=Jestice, Phyllis G. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=vrdZAAAAYAAJ&q=Emperor+%C5%8Cjin+historical+figure |title=Holy People of the World: A Cross-cultural Encyclopedia, Volumes 1-3 |publisher=ABC-CLIO |year=2004 |isbn=9781576073551 |page=653}}</ref><ref>{{cite book |author=Wakabayashi, Tadashi |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=LPvBrIQbj6QC&pg=PA108 |title=''Japanese loyalism reconstrued'' |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |year=1995 |isbn=9780824816674 |page=108}}</ref>

According to the ''Kiki'', Empress Jingū gave birth to a baby boy whom she named ''Homutawake'' (aka [[Emperor Ōjin]]) following her return from Korean conquest. The legend alleges that her son was conceived but unborn when Emperor Chūai died. As three more years would pass before ''Homutawake'' was finally born, this claim appears to be mythical and symbolic rather than real. Scholar [[William George Aston]] has suggested that this claim was misinterpreted, and instead refers to a period of less than nine months containing three "years" (some seasons), e.g. three [[Harvest|harvests]].<ref>[[William George Aston|Aston, William]]. (1998). ''Nihongi'', Vol. 1, pp. 224–253.</ref>

<ref name=":0">[[William George Aston|Aston, William]]. (1998). ''Nihongi'', Vol. 1, pp. 224–253.</ref>
<ref name=":0" />
<ref>[[William George Aston|Aston, William George]]. (1998). ''Nihongi'', p. 254–271.</ref>

<ref>{{cite book |author=Packard, Jerrold M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=z1ADAAAACAAJ&q=Sons+of+Heaven:+A+Portrait+of+the+Japanese+Monarchy |title=''Sons of Heaven: A Portrait of the Japanese Monarchy'' |publisher=FireWord Publishing, Incorporated |year=2000 |isbn=9781930782013 |page=45}}</ref><ref name="Xinzhong">{{cite book |author=[[Xinzhong Yao|Xinzhong, Yao]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=oguhmCO7bZEC&q=Ch%C5%ABai+died&pg=PA467 |title=Confucianism O - Z |publisher=Taylor & Francis US |year=2003 |isbn=9780415306539 |page=467}}</ref>

<ref name="Chuai is the son of Yamato Takeru">{{cite book |author=Packard, Jerrold M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=z1ADAAAACAAJ&q=Sons+of+Heaven:+A+Portrait+of+the+Japanese+Monarchy |title=''Sons of Heaven: A Portrait of the Japanese Monarchy'' |publisher=FireWord Publishing, Incorporated |year=2000 |isbn=9781930782013 |page=45}}</ref><ref name="Xinzhong">{{cite book |author=[[Xinzhong Yao|Xinzhong, Yao]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=oguhmCO7bZEC&q=Ch%C5%ABai+died&pg=PA467 |title=Confucianism O - Z |publisher=Taylor & Francis US |year=2003 |isbn=9780415306539 |page=467}}</ref>

<ref name="Chuai is the son of Yamato Takeru">{{cite book |author=Packard, Jerrold M. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=z1ADAAAACAAJ&q=Sons+of+Heaven:+A+Portrait+of+the+Japanese+Monarchy |title=''Sons of Heaven: A Portrait of the Japanese Monarchy'' |publisher=FireWord Publishing, Incorporated |year=2000 |isbn=9781930782013 |page=45}}</ref><ref name="Xinzhong">{{cite book |author=[[Xinzhong Yao|Xinzhong, Yao]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=oguhmCO7bZEC&q=Ch%C5%ABai+died&pg=PA467 |title=Confucianism O - Z |publisher=Taylor & Francis US |year=2003 |isbn=9780415306539 |page=467}}</ref>
<ref name="Chuai is the son of Yamato Takeru" /><ref name="Xinzhong" />
<ref>Aston, William. (1998). ''Nihongi'', Vol. 1, pp. 254–271.</ref>

Emperor Suizei parentage<ref>{{cite book |author=[[Louis-Frédéric|Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&q=Suizei&pg=PA32 |title=''Japan Encyclopedia'' |publisher=Harvard University Press |year=2002 |isbn=9780674017535 |page=32}}</ref>.<ref name="Fane">{{cite book |author=[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=Annei |title=''The Imperial House of Japan'' |publisher=Ponsonby Memorial Society |year=1959 |page=29 & 418}}</ref><ref name="Brown 251">{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}</ref>
The ''Kojiki'' records that he ruled from the palace of {{Nihongo||片塩浮穴宮|[[Ukena-no-miya]]|extra=and in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as {{Nihongo2|片塩浮孔宮}}}} at Katashiro in [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi]] in what would come to be known as [[Yamato Province]].<ref name="Brown 251" /> During Emperor Annei's alleged lifetime, he had one wife named "{{Ill|Nunasokonakatsu-hime|ja|渟名底仲媛}}" and fathered three children with her. Annei's reign lasted from 549 BC until his death in 511 BC, his second or third son then took the throne and would later be referred to as [[Emperor Itoku]].
<ref name="Brown 251" /><ref>{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}}</ref>

Emperor Suizei parentage<ref name=":0">{{cite book |author=[[Louis-Frédéric|Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&q=Suizei&pg=PA32 |title=''Japan Encyclopedia'' |publisher=Harvard University Press |year=2002 |isbn=9780674017535 |page=32}}</ref>.<ref name="Fane">{{cite book |author=[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=Annei |title=''The Imperial House of Japan'' |publisher=Ponsonby Memorial Society |year=1959 |page=29 & 418}}</ref><ref name="Brown 251">{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}</ref>
<ref name=":0" /><ref name="Fane" /><ref name="Brown 251" />
The ''Kojiki'' records that he ruled from the palace of {{Nihongo||片塩浮穴宮|[[Ukena-no-miya]]|extra=and in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as {{Nihongo2|片塩浮孔宮}}}} at Katashiro in [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi]] in what would come to be known as [[Yamato Province]].<ref name="Brown 251" /> During Emperor Annei's alleged lifetime, he had one wife named "{{Ill|Nunasokonakatsu-hime|ja|渟名底仲媛}}" and fathered three children with her. Annei's reign lasted from 549 BC until his death in 511 BC, his second or third son then took the throne and would later be referred to as [[Emperor Itoku]].
<ref name="Brown 251" /><ref>{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}}</ref>

Emperor Suizei parentage<ref name="Suizei:0">{{cite book |author=[[Louis-Frédéric|Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&q=Suizei&pg=PA32 |title=''Japan Encyclopedia'' |publisher=Harvard University Press |year=2002 |isbn=9780674017535 |page=32}}</ref><ref name="Fane">{{cite book |author=[[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane, Richard]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=Annei |title=''The Imperial House of Japan'' |publisher=Ponsonby Memorial Society |year=1959 |page=29 & 418}}</ref><ref name="Brown 251">{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Katsuragi |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=251}}</ref>
<ref name="Suizei:0" /><ref name="Fane" /><ref name="Brown 251" />
The ''Kojiki'' records that he ruled from the palace of {{Nihongo||片塩浮穴宮|[[Ukena-no-miya]]|extra=and in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' as {{Nihongo2|片塩浮孔宮}}}} at Katashiro in [[Kawachi Province|Kawachi]] in what would come to be known as [[Yamato Province]].<ref name="Brown 251" /> During Emperor Annei's alleged lifetime, he had one wife named "{{Ill|Nunasokonakatsu-hime|ja|渟名底仲媛}}" and fathered three children with her. Annei's reign lasted from 549 BC until his death in 511 BC, his second or third son then took the throne and would later be referred to as [[Emperor Itoku]].
<ref name="Brown 251" /><ref>{{cite book |author=[[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&pg=PA4 |title=Nihon Ōdai Ichiran |publisher=Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland |year=1834 |page=4 |language=fr}}</ref>

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]Replaced content with '<ref name=":10">Anston, p. 144 (Vol. 1)</ref> <ref name=":102">Anston, p. 144 (Vol. 1)</ref>'
revision.text:
<ref name=":10">Anston, p. 144 (Vol. 1)</ref>
<ref name=":102">Anston, p. 144 (Vol. 1)</ref>

<ref name=":102">Anston, p. 144 (Vol. 1)</ref>
<ref name=":102" />

<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Kuwashi Hime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kuwashi-hime/ |access-date=2023-11-17 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}}</ref>
<ref name=":0" /> <references />

revision.comment: Replaced [[Wikipedia:VisualEditor/Named references|VE ref names]] using [[User:Nardog/RefRenamer|RefRenamer]]
revision.text:
<ref name=". A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史">{{Cite web |title=Kuwashi Hime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/kuwashi-hime/ |access-date=2023-11-17 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}}</ref>
<ref name=". A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史" /> <references />
<ref name="Utsushikome">Anston, p. 149 (Vol. 1)</ref>

<ref name="aston150–164">{{cite book |author=Aston, William George. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=_oEfAAAAYAAJ |title=Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697, Volume 2 |date=1896 |publisher=The Japan Society London |isbn=9780524053478 |pages=150–164 |author-link=William George Aston}}</ref>
<ref name="aston150–164" />

<ref name="Brown12">{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Emperor+Sujin |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=248 & 253}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |last=Henshall |first=Kenneth |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Sujin+148&pg=PA487 |title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945 |date=2013-11-07 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |isbn=978-0-8108-7872-3 |language=en}}</ref>

<ref name="Brown12">{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Emperor+Sujin |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=248 & 253}}</ref><ref name="Sujin-book">{{Cite book |last=Henshall |first=Kenneth |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Sujin+148&pg=PA487 |title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945 |date=2013-11-07 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |isbn=978-0-8108-7872-3 |language=en}}</ref>

<ref name="Brown1">{{cite book |author=[[Delmer Brown|Brown, Delmer M.]] and Ichirō Ishida |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=w4f5FrmIJKIC&q=Emperor+Suinin |title=A Translation and Study of the Gukanshō, an Interpretative History of Japan Written in 1219 |publisher=University of California Press |year=1979 |isbn=9780520034600 |page=248 & 253–254}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite book |last=Henshall |first=Kenneth |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=tmYYAgAAQBAJ&q=Suinin+69&pg=PA487 |title=Historical Dictionary of Japan to 1945 |date=2013-11-07 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |isbn=978-0-8108-7872-3 |language=en}}</ref>
<ref name="Brown1" /><ref name=":0" />

<ref>''[https://books.google.com/books?id=uxUUAQAAMAAJ&q=Otarashihiko-no-mikoto Memoirs of the Research Department of the Toyo Bunko (the Oriental Library), Issues 32-34]''. [[Toyo Bunko]]. 1974. p. 63. Retrieved July 30, 2019.</ref>

<ref name="Abe">{{cite book |author=Asakawa, Kan'ichi |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=K1MuAAAAYAAJ&q=K%C5%8Dgen |title=''The Early Institutional Life of Japan'' |publisher=Tokyo Shueisha |year=1903 |isbn=9780722225394 |page=140 |author-link=Kan'ichi Asakawa}}</ref>
<ref name="Abe" />

<ref name=":0">Louis-Frédéric, [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA513&dq= "Kibitsu-hiko no Mikoto"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 513.</ref>
<ref name=":0" />

<ref>{{Cite web |title=Nunasoko Nakatsuhime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/nunasoko-nakatsuhime/ |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}}</ref>

<ref>{{Cite web |title=Mimakihime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/mimakihime/ |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}}</ref>

<ref>{{Cite web |title=Sahobime • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/sahobime/ |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}}</ref>

{{Quote tweet|name=The Artist Formerly Known as Eric|username=breakingbaht|date=Nov 15, 2023|text=Okay.
Jewish communties have been pushing the exact kind of dialectical hatred against whites that they claim to want people to stop using against them.
I'm deeply disinterested in giving the tiniest shit now about western Jewish populations coming to the disturbing realization that those hordes of minorities that support flooding their country don't exactly like them too much.
You want truth said to your face, there it is.|ID=1724892505647296620|name2=Charles Weber|username2=CWBOCA|date2=Nov 15, 2023|text2=To the cowards hiding behind the anonymity of the internet and posting "Hitler was right":
You got something you want to say? Why dont you say it to our faces…|ID2=1724868476861301153}}

{{Tweet | name = [[Elon Musk]] | username = elonmusk | date = Nov 15, 2023 | text = You have said the actual truth | ID = 1724908287471272299 }}{{Quote tweet|name=The Artist Formerly Known as Eric|username=breakingbaht|date=Nov 15, 2023|text=Okay.
Jewish communties have been pushing the exact kind of dialectical hatred against whites that they claim to want people to stop using against them.
I'm deeply disinterested in giving the tiniest shit now about western Jewish populations coming to the disturbing realization that those hordes of minorities that support flooding their country don't exactly like them too much.
You want truth said to your face, there it is.|ID=1724892505647296620|name2=Charles Weber|username2=CWBOCA|date2=Nov 15, 2023|text2=To the cowards hiding behind the anonymity of the internet and posting "Hitler was right":
You got something you want to say? Why dont you say it to our faces…|ID2=1724868476861301153}}

Jewish communties have been pushing the exact kind of dialectical hatred against whites that they claim to want people to stop using against them.
I'm deeply disinterested in giving the tiniest shit now about western Jewish populations coming to the disturbing realization that those hordes of minorities that support flooding their country don't exactly like them too much.
You want truth said to your face, there it is.|ID=1724892505647296620|name2=Charles Weber|username2=CWBOCA|date2=Nov 15, 2023|text2=To the cowards hiding behind the anonymity of the internet and posting "Hitler was right":
You got something you want to say? Why dont you say it to our faces…|ID2=1724868476861301153}}

Jewish communties have been pushing the exact kind of dialectical hatred against whites that they claim to want people to stop using against them.
I'm deeply disinterested in giving the tiniest shit now about western Jewish populations coming to the disturbing realization that those hordes of minorities that support flooding their country don't exactly like them too much.
You want truth said to your face, there it is.|ID=1724892505647296620|name2=Charles Weber|username2=CWBOCA|date2=Nov 15, 2023|text2=To the cowards hiding behind the anonymity of the internet and posting "Hitler was right":
You got something you want to say? Why dont you say it to our faces…|ID2=1724868476861301153}} {{Tweet | name = [[Elon Musk]] | username = elonmusk | date = Nov 15, 2023 | text = You have said the actual truth | ID = 1724908287471272299 }}

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]Replaced content with 'ewrt'
revision.text: ewrt

=== M ===
* [[Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]]
=== S ===
* [[Sagami-ji]] * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] * [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)]] * [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]] * [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] * [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]] * [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)|lt=|simple||ja|住吉神社 (壱岐市)}}

revision.comment: removed [[Category:Sumiyoshi Shrines]]; added [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] using [[WP:HC|HotCat]]
Immanuelle moved page [[User:Immanuelle/sandbox]] to [[Template:Sumiyoshi Faith]]
repair poor linking
revision.text:
{{Navbox | name = Sumiyoshi Faith | title = [[Sumiyoshi Taisha|Sumiyoshi Faith]] | listclass = hlist | image = [[File:Itsukushima Shrine Torii Gate (13890465459).jpg|150px|link=|alt=]] |group1 = Main Deity |list1 = * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]]
|group2 = Head Shrine |list2 = * [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]]
|group3 = Other Shrines |list3 = * [[Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * [[Sagami-ji]] * [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)]] * [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]] * [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] * [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]]
|group4= Misc |list4= * [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]]
}}<noinclude>

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Template:Sumiyoshi Faith]] to [[Template:Sumiyoshi and Watatsumi Faiths]]

revision.text:
{{Navbox | name = Sumiyoshi Faith | title = [[Sumiyoshi Taisha|Sumiyoshi Faith]] and [[Watatsumi|Watatsumi Faith]] | listclass = hlist | image = [[File:Itsukushima Shrine Torii Gate (13890465459).jpg|150px|link=|alt=]] |group1 = Main Deity |list1 = * [[Sumiyoshi sanjin]] * [[Watatsumi]] |group2 = Head Shrine |list2 = * [[Sumiyoshi-taisha]] |group3 = Other Shrines |list3 = * [[Moto-Sumiyoshi Shrine]] * [[Sagami-ji]] * [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)]] * [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Hokkaidō)]] * [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|simple||ja|5=住吉神社 (壱岐市)}} |group4= list4= [[Kaijin Shrine]],
{{ill|Watatsumi jinja|ja|海神社 (神戸市)|simple|Watatsumi Shrine}},
[[Shikaumi Shrine]],
{{ill|Watazumi Shrine|ja| 和多都美神社 |draft}} |group5= Misc |list5= * [[Sumiyoshi-zukuri]] }} [[Category:Shinto templates]] [[Category:Sumiyoshi shrines]] [[Category:Shinto cult templates]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Template:Sumiyoshi and Watatsumi Faiths]] to [[Template:Watatsumi and Sumiyoshi Faiths]]
Convert [[Watazumi Shrine]] to wikilink (The bot operation is completed 78.2% in total)
Disambiguating links to [[Watatsumi Shrine]] (intentional link to DAB) using [[User:Qwertyytrewqqwerty/DisamAssist|DisamAssist]].
Disambiguating links to [[Sumiyoshi Shrine]] (intentional link to DAB) using [[User:Qwertyytrewqqwerty/DisamAssist|DisamAssist]].
Convert [[Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)]], [[Furogu Shrine]] to wikilink (The bot operation is completed 80.3% in total)
Convert [[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)|Sumiyoshi Shrine]], [[Hotaka Shrine]], [[Nunakuma Shrine]] to wikilink (The bot operation is completed 90.6% in total)
77 revisions imported from [[:wikipedia:Template:Watatsumi_and_Sumiyoshi_Faiths]]

Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates

Text replacement - "|qq=|" to "|"
Text replacement - "|qq=draft|" to "|"

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "Utsushihikanasaku" to "Utsushi Hikanasaku no Mikoto"
